page: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the e2 family. It is configured with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance runs on an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. This processor features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core, and utilizes dedicated CPU allocation. The cache hierarchy includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-assisted virtualization, AES cryptography, and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), though it lacks AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a mixed performance profile. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and Passmark CPU scores fall within the average range. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput of 7.58 GB/sec align with average tier expectations. Conversely, the instance exhibits poor performance in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. Software compilation and image processing benchmarks are weak, falling in the bottom 25%. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance achieves average speeds of 443.82 tokens per second for prompt processing and 161.43 tokens per second for text generation using a small 135M parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-standard-8 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-performance computing or local NVMe storage. Given its average multi-core capabilities and moderate memory bandwidth, it is well-suited for standard web serving, Redis caching, and moderate database operations. It is less optimal for compute-intensive tasks like heavy software compilation, data compression, or complex text processing due to its lower performance in those specific benchmarks.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, it features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading and dedicated CPU allocation. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a mixed profile: stress-ng single-core performance is strong, while multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, Redis operations, and static web serving throughput are average. However, it performs poorly in single-threaded compression and text processing tasks. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard, non-intensive workloads, making it suitable for general-purpose applications, moderate database operations, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced performance for standard web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is an 8-vCPU, 32 GB RAM general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and moderate memory density for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM running on x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture
Dedicated CPU allocation with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading
Strong single-core CPU performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for general-purpose web serving, Redis caching, and moderate databases
